message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Fix: Change notification badge color from red to amber

## Context
The notification badge (red circle with unread count) on the Notifications tab icon uses the React Navigation theme's `notification` color, which is currently set to `colors.danger` (red). This clashes with the app's amber/gold accent theme. The user wants it to match the rest of the app.

## Change

**File:** `apps/mobile/src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx` (line 189)

Change:
```tsx
notification: colors.danger,
```
To:
```tsx
notification: colors.accentAmber,
```

This single change updates the badge color from red (`#EF4444` dark / `#DC2626` light) to amber (`#E0BA6A` dark / `#D4A853` light), matching the app's accent color used throughout the UI.

## Verification
- Rebuild and run the app on device/emulator
- Navigate to the Notifications tab — the badge circle should now be amber/gold instead of red


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\johnm\.claude\projects\C--Users-johnm-iskrib-mobile-app\3080c4cf-835f-4b66-abc0-3a1b6325ec2f.jsonl

planContent:
# Fix: Change notification badge color from red to amber

## Context
The notification badge (red circle with unread count) on the Notifications tab icon uses the React Navigation theme's `notification` color, which is currently set to `colors.danger` (red). This clashes with the app's amber/gold accent theme. The user wants it to match the rest of the app.

## Change

**File:** `apps/mobile/src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx` (line 189)

Change:
```tsx
notification: colors.danger,
```
To:
```tsx
notification: colors.accentAmber,
```

This single change updates the badge color from red (`#EF4444` dark / `#DC2626` light) to amber (`#E0BA6A` dark / `#D4A853` light), matching the app's accent color used throughout the UI.

## Verification
- Rebuild and run the app on device/emulator
- Navigate to the Notifications tab — the badge circle should now be amber/gold instead of red

message.content: what i mean is why no .notification-count on the notification icon-bell

content.thinking: The user is asking about why there's no notification count displayed on the notification bell icon. Let me look at how the notification tab icon is configured to understand the current implementation.

content.text: Let me look at how the notification tab icon is set up.
I see — the bell icon has no badge showing the unread count. Let me check if there's an unread count available and how the tab options are configured.

content.content:
Found 11 files

content.thinking: Now I understand the issue. The Notifications tab at line 122-127 has no `tabBarBadge` set, so there's no unread count showing on the bell icon. I need to look at what's available for unread notification counts — let me check the NotificationsScreen and the API to see if there's an unread count mechanism.

content.text: I see — the Notifications tab (line 126) only renders the bell icon with no `tabBarBadge`. There's no unread count badge at all. Let me check what's available for fetching unread counts.

content.thinking:
Now I understand. The `NotificationsScreen` already fetches `unreadCountQuery` (line 93-97) and displays it as a badge inside the screen header (line 193-197), but the tab bar icon itself has no `tabBarBadge`. 

The `MainTabNavigator` at line 122-127 just shows the bell icon with no badge. To show the unread count on the tab bar bell icon, we need to:

1. Get the unread count in the `MainTabNavigator` (or pass it down)
2. Set `tabBarBadge` on the Notifications screen options

The challenge is that the unread count query is currently inside `NotificationsScreen`, not available at the navigator level. 

I should move the unread count query up to the `MainTabNavigator` level so I can set it as the `tabBarBadge` directly. I'll need to use `useAuth` to get the token and userId, then call `socialApi.getNotificationsCount` to fetch the count there.

content.text:
Got it. The unread count is already fetched inside `NotificationsScreen` (line 93-97) and shown as a badge in the screen header, but it's **not** wired to the tab bar icon. The tab bar bell icon at line 126 has no `tabBarBadge`.

The fix is to query the unread count in `MainTabNavigator` and set `tabBarBadge` on the Notifications tab. Let me check the API shape quickly.
Now I have everything I need. I'll add the unread count query to `MainTabNavigator` and wire it to `tabBarBadge`.

content.content: The file C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\navigation\RootNavigator.tsx has been updated successfully.

content.text: Now update `MainTabNavigator` to query the unread count and set the badge:

content.content: The file C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\src\navigation\RootNavigator.tsx has been updated successfully.

content.text: Now add the `tabBarBadge` to the Notifications tab:

input.old_string:
<MainTabs.Screen
        name="Notifications"
        component={NotificationsScreen}
        options={{
          tabBarIcon: ({color, size}) => <BellIcon size={size} color={color} />,
        }}
      />
input.new_string:
<MainTabs.Screen
        name="Notifications"
        component={NotificationsScreen}
        options={{
          tabBarIcon: ({color, size}) => <BellIcon size={size} color={color} />,
          tabBarBadge: unreadCount > 0 ? unreadCount : undefined,
          tabBarBadgeStyle: {
            backgroundColor: colors.accentAmber,
            color: '#1A1612',
            fontSize: 11,
            fontWeight: '700',
          },
        }}
      />

        options={{
          tabBarIcon: ({color, size}) => <BellIcon size={size} color={color} />,
          tabBarBadge: unreadCount > 0 ? unreadCount : undefined,
          tabBarBadgeStyle: {
            backgroundColor: colors.accentAmber,

            fontSize: 11,
            fontWeight: '700',
          },
        }}
      />

content.text: Let me verify the final result:

Looks good. Here's what changed in `RootNavigator.tsx`:

1. **Added imports** (lines 10, 14): `useQuery` from React Query and `socialApi` for the count endpoint.

2. **Added unread count query** (lines 83-93) in `MainTabNavigator`: Fetches the unread notification count using the same `['notification-count', userId]` query key that `NotificationsScreen` already uses (so they share cache). Polls every 30 seconds.

3. **Added `tabBarBadge`** (lines 141-147) on the Notifications tab: Shows the unread count as an amber badge on the bell icon. The badge only appears when `unreadCount > 0`, styled with the amber background and dark text to match the app's theme.

The badge will also auto-clear when the user reads notifications, since `NotificationsScreen` already invalidates the `['notification-count', userId]` query on mark-read and delete.
